_instructions.usage: Fill in every field below with real project data, then seed it with: npx tsx scripts/seed-from-json.ts path/to/this-file.json
rules: Do NOT remove any key — if unknown, leave as null or empty string.
Leave image/document storage_url and url as empty string '' — add via admin Media panel

project.name: Sikka Karmic Greens
project.address: Plot No. 01C, Sector 78, Noida, Uttar Pradesh 201305
project.tagline: Elegant Golf Living Mired in Regulatory Dispute
project.description: A ready-to-move-in residential society featuring elegant layouts designed by C.P. Kukreja Associates, but severely mired in massive Noida Authority dues disputes.
project.long_description: Sikka Karmic Greens (developed by G S Promoters Private Limited under Sikka Group) is a physically completed residential development located in Sector 78, Noida. The project sits on a portion of a 25-acre master plan, housing 616/844 apartments across 6 high-rise towers. It was designed by the esteemed C.P. Kukreja Associates with an elegant golf-living aesthetic and 70% open area. However, behind the beautiful physical structures lies a severe regulatory crisis: the developer owes over Rs 90 Crore to the Noida Authority and defaulted on the 25% upfront payment required under the UP Government's rehabilitation scheme. This has resulted in a complete registry freeze where only 98 flats have been registered, leaving 477 families occupying unregistered homes. In early 2025, an FIR was filed against the developer for erasing outstanding dues details on a public notice board with black ink, adding a layers of criminal investigation and deep title anxiety for prospective buyers.
marketing_claims: Designed by India's top architects C.P. Kukreja
project.project_risk_flag: Extreme Risk: Developer GS Promoters owes Rs 90 Crore in land dues, triggering a registry embargo. Only 98 flats registered; ex-management facing FIR and Delhi Police EOW actions.
registry_embargo_reasons: Outstanding land dues of Rs 90 Crore to the Noida Authority
Developer defaulted on upfront payments under rehabilitation schemes
FIR registered against GS Promoters for erasing public notice board dues with black ink

builder.name: Sikka Group (GS Promoters)
builder.company_overview: Sikka Group (operating project SPVs under G S Promoters Private Limited) is a mid-sized NCR builder established over three decades ago. While the builder successfully delivered multiple early-phase housing schemes, their late-stage projects have been mired in extreme debt defaults, land dues delays, and severe Noida Authority actions including Recovery Certificates (RCs).

unit_types.subtitle: Smart 2 BHK with compact dimensions

unit_types.subtitle: Comfortable 2 BHK with separate study room

unit_types.subtitle: Grand 3 BHK featuring helper study utility
unit_types.description: Premium 3 BHK layout featuring three bathrooms, servant study utility, and park-facing balconies.

assumptions: GST is physically 0% because towers are built, but registries are mired in a total embargo.
Buyers must negotiate hard with resale owners due to massive outstanding builder land debt.

payment_plan.notes: Standard bank mortgages are blocked. Purchases must be self-funded with deep cash pools.

milestones.milestone: Full Payment / Handover of Keys (Registry pending on NOC)

decision_profile.decision_thesis: Sikka Karmic Greens represents an extreme-risk value trap, offering physically complete golf-theme apartments at massive price discounts mired under a total Noida Authority registry freeze and active criminal FIR investigations.
why_buy: Severe price discount: Sells at nearly ₹8,500/sqft, offering massive spatial value in a mature central sector.
Physical readiness: Towers are completed and highly occupied, with zero waiting on construction.
Beautiful design layout envisioned by C.P. Kukreja Associates.
why_avoid: Absolute Registry Embargo: The builder owes over ₹1,717 Cr (Eco City consortium) and was booked under an FIR in early 2025 for erasing public notice board dues with black ink.
No Bank Funding: Institutional lenders refuse mortgages due to the complete lack of registry and active Noida Authority recovery certificates (RC).
Only 98 of 616 units are legally registered, leaving 477 families occupying unregistered properties.
decision_profile.best_for: Highly risk-tolerant cash buyers or yield-seeking investors comfortable with secondary un-registered transfers.
decision_profile.not_ideal_for: Conservative nuclear families, first-time homebuyers requiring safe bank mortgages, or short-term flippers.

buyerPersonas.headline: High Risk Registry Arbitrage
reasons: Acquiring physical assets at near ₹8,500/sqft, hoping for future political wave-offs on builder dues.
Strong tenant pool generates cash rental income without active titles.

riskRadar.description: Complete registry embargo. Outstanding land dues of Rs 90 Crore. Ex-management booked under criminal FIR.

transparency_checks.label: OC / CC Received
transparency_checks.details: Noida Authority dues block final CC/OC.

transparency_checks.label: Registry Active
transparency_checks.details: Completely frozen due to outstanding dues.

investment_insights.appreciation_desc: Capital values are heavily depressed by title uncertainty and regulatory actions.
investment_insights.rental_desc: Consistent rental cashflow from IT sector tenants despite zero property registry.

persona_profile.income_range: Rs 30L+ per annum (must hold independent self-funding cash channels)
persona_profile.risk_appetite: Aggressive / Extreme (willing to bypass legal registries for spatial discount)
persona_profile.timeline_horizon: 10+ Years (requires long horizon to resolve state-level defaults)
persona_profile.motivation_note: Driven entirely by cheap price per square foot and immediate physical move-in availability, disregarding standard regulatory protections.

recommendation_profile.primary_thesis: Sikka Karmic Greens represents an extreme title-ownership hazard. Despite its attractive CP Kukreja architectural layouts and cheap pricing, the outstanding land dues of ₹90 Crore, combined with developer criminal investigations (FIR), make this a critical risk. Avoid completely.
recommendation_profile.end_use_thesis: From an end-user perspective, while you can physically reside in the apartments, you will not possess a legally valid sub-lease deed. This blocks any future bank resale, leaving you vulnerable to potential Noida Authority asset attachments.
recommendation_profile.investment_thesis: Speculators might find interest in the cheap ₹8,500/sqft base price, but the absolute lack of liquidity and severe legal distress cap any legitimate capital appreciation.
recommendation_profile.family_thesis: Highly discouraged for families. Gated common area infrastructure suffers from deferred builder maintenance, and active litigation causes perpetual community anxiety.
recommendation_profile.investor_thesis: Only useful for extreme cash-rich investors looking to extract rent from IT professionals without caring about safe capital exit routes.
recommendation_profile.luxury_thesis: Thematic golf design is physically beautiful, but final tower finish quality is compromised due to late-stage builder liquidity crunches.
recommendation_profile.risk_thesis: Extreme legal risk. Outstanding municipal debt, blocked registries for 477 flats, and active Delhi Police EOW investigations create a highly toxic title trail.
walk_away_conditions: Any transaction requiring standard home loans (mortgages are 100% blocked).
Failure of the seller to present a bipartite registry clearance under the 98 exempt registered flats.
negotiation_leverage: Use the criminal FIR of 2025/2026 and the complete land dues crisis to slash seller pricing by at least 40% in cash transactions.
recommendation_profile.timeline_advice: Do not buy unless the state government executes a complete sovereign waiver of builder land dues, or the society is legally handed over to an active AOA under court orders.

competitors.this_project_advantage: Extremely low entry cost, giving a larger 3 BHK footprint for the price of a compact 2 BHK in Hyde Park.
competitors.competitor_advantage: 100% legal title safety, active bipartite registries, and full CC/OC with absolutely zero municipal debt distress.
competitors.verdict: IITL Nimbus wins handily on legal safety and transit reliability; Sikka wins only on cheap spatial pricing.
competitors.price_delta_note: Sikka is ₹2,000/sqft cheaper than Nimbus Hyde Park.
